title: Functional Core, Imperative Shell, and Production Integration · Equational Recall
summary: Rebuild an unreliable data-processing service as a pure decision core with explicit operational effects. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a production-shaped functional service and decision record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate pure domain decisions from I/O orchestration with explicit data contracts.
Pass clocks, randomness, storage, and transport behavior through narrow callable interfaces.
Compose ingestion, validation, transformation, aggregation, and emission with bounded resource use.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-functional immutable transformation portfolio."""

from collections.abc import Callable, Iterable, Iterator
from functools import partial, reduce
from itertools import chain, islice
from typing import TypeVar

T = TypeVar('T')
U = TypeVar('U')

# Extend one pure, lazy, tested pipeline increment per activity.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — A local decision core receives an injected clock and storage callable. Replaying the same 3-event fixture must reproduce decisions while I/O failures stay at the imperative boundary.

Inspect the Architecture Defense trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for defend functional and imperative boundaries using correctness, operability, and team-maintenance evidence.

content.text: For Architecture Defense, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Architecture Defense precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A local decision core receives an injected clock and storage callable. Replaying the same 3-event fixture must reproduce decisions while I/O failures stay at the imperative boundary.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Paradigm purity is more important than stakeholder outcomes.

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend functional and imperative boundaries using correctness, operability, and team-maintenance evidence. Artifact under review: a production-shaped functional service and decision record.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Architecture Defense, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Hide effects inside the transformation, mutate its input, and accept one plausible return value as proof of Architecture Defense.

steps.title: Record what required effort

Replaying the same 5-event fixture must reproduce decisions while I/O failures stay at the imperative boundary.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend functional and imperative boundaries using correctness, operability, and team-maintenance evidence.
